title: pinguicula vulgaris subsp. leptoceras
intro: Pinguicula vulgaris subsp. leptoceras, also known as the 'butterwort,' is a carnivorous plant species that obtains essential nutrients by capturing and digesting insects. Traditionally, Pinguicula vulgaris subsp. leptoceras has been used in folk medicine due to its reported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Pinguicula vulgaris subsp. leptoceras can be made by infusing the leaves in water, and it is often used topically to treat minor cuts and scrapes.
